_meta.title: The 30 transition hinges — the retrieval spine
_meta.role: One hinge per cross-element transition (no self-loops, no DOF). Each is a before → turn → after in plain experiential language, vocabulary stripped, so the retrieval gate works on the description alone. Drafted from maneuvers.json (source_ontology → destination_ontology, transition_signature, failure_mode — the failure gives the turn its friction). `sig` = a short plain label (never shown in a generated prompt). The hinge feeds the RETRIEVAL-PROMPT GENERATOR alongside an area-φ; whether it generates or DECLINEs depends on the area + source-type.
_meta.calibration: Wood→Air is Daniel's standard; the rest aim at it.

transitions.sig: from dissecting to feeling-with
transitions.hinge: Holding something at arm's length — naming it, taking it apart, keeping it clearly in view — until the dissecting quiets and you find yourself feeling *with* it instead of about it. Afterward it's known by resonance, not category: closer, and harder to stay separate from.

transitions.sig: from clear view to chosen stance
transitions.hinge: Turning a question over, seeing it more and more clearly, the analysis still open — until the seeing tips into a stance: 'therefore, this.' The clear view closes into a decision, and afterward the looking serves the aim rather than the other way around — sharp, committed, and tempted to stop questioning.

transitions.sig: from exact cut to open fork
transitions.hinge: Pinning a thing down, getting the distinctions exact — until one precise cut springs open into 'and what else could this be?' The naming turns to inventing; a single clear line becomes a fork, then many. Afterward you're generating variations, lighter and less sure where it lands.

transitions.sig: from grasped to second-nature
transitions.hinge: A clear understanding, freshly grasped and held in the mind — that sinks, through repetition, below thought, until it's no longer something you know but something you simply do. Afterward it runs on its own, out of awareness; the danger is that the living insight has become mere rote.

transitions.sig: from useful line to fixed rule
transitions.hinge: Drawing a clear, useful line — a distinction that helps — until the line is set down as a rule: what was a way of seeing becomes a boundary others must keep. Afterward it has authority and edges; the danger is the rule hardening past the reason it was drawn.

transitions.sig: from inside the feeling to naming it
transitions.hinge: Immersed in a feeling or a bond, inside it without edges — until you step back just enough to name what's actually happening: this is mine, that is theirs, this is the shape of it. Afterward you can speak it clearly, with some distance — the risk being that you cut so far back you go cold.

transitions.sig: from care to act on it
transitions.hinge: Feeling deeply for someone or something — moved, full of care with nowhere to put it — until the feeling finds a direction: 'I will do something about this.' The warmth becomes drive. Afterward you're acting, propelled by what you feel; the danger is the raw feeling hardening the action into a crusade.

transitions.sig: from closeness to invitation
transitions.hinge: Close to someone or something, attuned, resting in the connection — until the closeness opens a door: being-with sparks 'what could this become?' Intimacy turns to invitation. Afterward you're exploring, possibilities branching out of the bond — the risk being that you scatter and the closeness thins.

transitions.sig: from warm feeling to steady tending
transitions.hinge: A live, warm feeling of care — that settles into the daily doing of it: the cooking, the checking-in, the unglamorous upkeep. Feeling becomes feeding. Afterward the bond is sustained by steady tending — the danger being the tending outliving the warmth and becoming depleting duty.

transitions.sig: from open relating to agreed form
transitions.hinge: Relating openly, reading each other, fluid — until the relating takes a form: agreements, roles, 'this is how we do this.' Closeness builds itself a structure to keep it safe. Afterward it's reliable and boundaried — the danger being the form outlasting the feeling, leaving obligation where there was warmth.

transitions.sig: from driving to questioning the aim
transitions.hinge: Driving hard toward a goal, sure of it, moving — until the drive stops to ask what it's actually chasing: 'what is this for, and what am I really looking at?' Momentum yields to a clear, named view. Afterward the aim is examined — the danger being that the examining quietly becomes justification for the goal you'd already picked.

transitions.sig: from target to the people it costs
transitions.hinge: Driving hard toward a goal, narrowed to the target, the people around it a blur — until something gives: a cost lands, or a face breaks through, and the goal cracks open to what it has been doing to the people it touched. Afterward the same aim is still there, but held *through* who it's for and what it's costing them.

transitions.sig: from fixed path to reopened options
transitions.hinge: Locked on one path, committed, the goal fixed — until it loosens: 'maybe there are other ways through, other things this could even be.' The single aim reopens into options. Afterward you're holding alternatives again, lighter — the risk being you reopen so far the thread is lost.

transitions.sig: from burning fast to built to last
transitions.hinge: Burning toward something, fast, spending freely — until the pace breaks against its own cost: 'can this be kept up, and what is it burning?' The aim bends toward what can actually be sustained. Afterward it's slower and built to last — the danger being it settles so far into upkeep that the direction quietly goes out.

transitions.sig: from live drive to durable system
transitions.hinge: A live drive, a mission carried on momentum — until it builds itself a structure to outlast the heat: rules, roles, an institution to hold the aim when the energy flags. Afterward it runs as a durable system — the danger being the structure becoming a fortress that defends itself long after the purpose is forgotten.

transitions.sig: from playful what-if to totalizing lens
transitions.hinge: A loose, playful 'what if' — a possibility entertained for its own sake, lightly, without commitment — that tightens into the conviction that one now sees the underlying pattern, and begins reading that pattern into everything encountered afterward.

transitions.sig: from sampling to belonging
transitions.hinge: Playing among possibilities, trying things on lightly — until one of them catches and deepens: the trying-on becomes belonging-to, a sampled connection turns real. Afterward you're held in a bond that grew out of the play — the risk being you either keep sampling and never land, or land so hard you disappear into it.

transitions.sig: from many options to the one
transitions.hinge: Many options open, all alive, none chosen, enjoying the room — until one of them stops being one-of-many and becomes *the* one. The whole field collapses into a single choice and you commit. Afterward you're moving on one path, the rest released — the danger being you commit so hard the roads not taken start to feel like betrayals.

transitions.sig: from sprouting to cultivating
transitions.hinge: Generating freely, new shoots everywhere, starting things — until the starting gives way to tending: you stop sprouting new ideas and begin feeding the few that actually took root. Afterward you're cultivating and pruning — the danger being you grow so careful that nothing new is ever started again.

transitions.sig: from emerging to fixed form
transitions.hinge: Something emerging, half-formed and fluid, still becoming — until you give it a definite shape: the open possibility is fixed into a form, a spec, a finished thing. Afterward it's solid and usable — the danger being you fix it so firmly the life goes out of it, leaving form with no give.

transitions.sig: from habit to seeing the habit
transitions.hinge: Doing the thing by habit, hands knowing it, no thought needed — until something in the doing surfaces into awareness and you suddenly see what you've actually been doing all along. Afterward you can name it and look at it from outside — the danger being you examine it so much you can no longer simply do it.

transitions.sig: from upkeep to felt care again
transitions.hinge: Steadily maintaining something, doing the upkeep, a little dutiful — until, in the tending, the feeling comes back: the chore becomes felt care, duty warms into connection again. Afterward you tend from warmth, the bond alive — the risk being you melt so far into the feeling that the steadiness slips.

transitions.sig: from keeping-going to a chosen want
transitions.hinge: Just keeping going — maintaining, recovering, doing the next necessary thing with no direction — until out of the steadiness a genuine want surfaces: not an obligation, an actual pull toward something. Afterward a direction is chosen from restored footing, moved toward because it's wanted, not because it must be done.

transitions.sig: from composting to new shoots
transitions.hinge: An ending — a loss, something quietly composting, the slow rot of what's done — until, out of the breakdown, new shoots appear: the decay turns out to have been feeding something. What ended becomes the ground for what's starting. Afterward there's new growth rising from the old — the danger being you reach for the shoots before the old has finished breaking down.

transitions.sig: from living rhythm to fixed system
transitions.hinge: A living rhythm — a practice that renews itself, breathing in cycles — until it's fixed into a system: the cycle captured as a schedule, a protocol, a fixed form. Afterward it's reliable and holds the rhythm steady — the danger being the form stiffening until it kills the living rhythm it was built to keep.

transitions.sig: from kept rule to opened question
transitions.hinge: Holding a firm structure, a rule kept without question, solid underfoot — until the rule is opened up: 'why is this here, what does it actually do?' The fixed boundary becomes a question again. Afterward you understand it rather than merely keep it — the danger being you take it apart so far there's nothing left to stand on.

transitions.sig: from held line to felt connection
transitions.hinge: Holding a firm line — a rule, a boundary, a non-negotiable — to stay intact, keeping something out. Then the line is pressed by a relationship that matters, and instead of defending it one feels what it has been excluding. Afterward the boundary is still there but porous to the connection it was walling off — held differently.

transitions.sig: from static form to aimed force
transitions.hinge: A solid structure, held, static, in place — until it's taken up and aimed: the form becomes a platform to push from, the kept integrity converts into drive. Afterward you're moving with force, the structure serving a direction — the danger being the old rigidity hardening the new drive into a crusade.

transitions.sig: from cage to trellis
transitions.hinge: A fixed form, a finished structure, settled — until it turns from cage to trellis: the firm thing becomes something to grow new things *on*, the boundary enabling rather than only containing. Afterward new growth climbs the old structure — the danger being either the structure cracks under it, or the growth runs past anything it can hold.

transitions.sig: from defended to renewed
transitions.hinge: A held boundary, a kept structure, maintained as-is — until keeping it becomes feeding it: the static thing turns into something that has to be renewed, not just defended. Afterward it's sustained through ongoing care — the danger being the care sliding into mere preservation, the structure embalmed rather than alive.
